title: Missing XML namespace in Error responses for AWS S3 compatibility
description: The AwsError::to_xml() method in crates/cirrus-protocol/src/error.rs is missing the AWS S3 XML namespace declaration (xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/") in Error responses. This causes incompatibility with AWS SDKs that expect the namespace to be present. The fix involves adding the xmlns attribute to the root Error element in the XML output.
design: Modify the to_xml() method in crates/cirrus-protocol/src/error.rs to add the XML namespace declaration to the Error element
acceptance_criteria: Error responses include xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/" attribute on the root Error element
close_reason: Added XML namespace declaration to Error responses in AwsError::to_xml() method. The fix ensures AWS S3 compatibility by including xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/" attribute on the root Error element. All tests pass.

title: AP-P11: Service handler errors silently swallowed — no logging
notes: DON'T convert service.handle() Err to HTTP response without logging — operators see 501 with zero server-side trace. DO: Add tracing::error! with service name, error_code, status before aws_error_response(). Fix location: crates/cirrus-router/src/service.rs L155. PR#6 review finding (bug-hunter + code-quality-reviewer).
close_reason: Fixed: added tracing::error! logging before aws_error_response() with service_name, error_code, and status. Build, tests (11 passed), and clippy all clean.

title: fix: reduce lock contention in complete_multipart_upload
description: Scope DashMap::RefMut to validation only; collect Vec<Bytes> (O(1) clone) during validation, drop lock, then concatenate/hash/create object outside the lock
close_reason: Fix applied and verified: all 31 tests pass, clippy clean

title: delete_bucket re-insertion race with concurrent create_bucket
description:
delete_bucket (L345-360) removes the bucket via DashMap::remove(), checks if empty, then re-inserts if BucketNotEmpty. Between remove() and re-insert(), a concurrent create_bucket with the same name could pass the contains_key check and insert a new bucket, which would then be silently overwritten by the re-insert.

**Location:** crates/cirrus-s3/src/storage.rs L348-356

**Fix options:**
1. Use DashMap::entry() API for atomic check-and-insert
2. Accept as acceptable for in-memory test backend (document limitation)
3. Add a per-bucket mutex for create/delete serialization

**Severity:** Low for in-memory backend, would be critical for production storage.

Discovered during PR #5 review. Depends on cirrus-qhv.
close_reason: Fixed by changing re-insert from insert() to entry().or_insert() — DashMap atomic entry API prevents concurrent create_bucket from being silently overwritten. Verification: cargo check/clippy/test all pass. PR #5 continues to protect.

title: complete_multipart_upload holds RefMut across validation+concatenation
close_reason: Fixed: scoped DashMap RefMut to validation+collection block. Lock hold time reduced from O(data_size) to O(validation). Verified: cargo check/clippy/test all pass (31 tests). PR diff in feat/cirrus-s3t.

title: Weak assertions in CopyObjectResult test
description: test_copy_object_result_serialize (L580) only checks <ETag> and <LastModified> exist, not their values. These are the only two fields, so test wouldn't catch value corruption.
close_reason: Fixed weak assertions in CopyObjectResult test - strengthened assertions to validate actual values, not just existence

title: Redundant #[serde(default)] on Option<String>
description: Line 229: #[serde(default)] on Option<String> is unnecessary — Option already defaults to None when absent.
notes: Fixed: Removed redundant #[serde(default)] from Option<String> field version_id in DeleteObject struct (line 419). Since Option<String> already defaults to None when absent, the serde(default) attribute was unnecessary.
close_reason: Removed redundant #[serde(default)] from Option<String> field in DeleteObject struct. The fix has been tested with cargo check and clippy with no errors.

title: .expect() panic path in library code
description: to_xml_string uses .expect() at L65 which panics if serialization ever fails. Library code should return Result and let callers decide error strategy.
close_reason: Fixed .expect() panic by returning Result type and letting callers handle errors

title: PartNumber lacks range validation (1-10000)
description: Part.part_number (L300) allows values outside 1-10000 range. Spec requires part numbers 1-10000. Type permits construction of invalid requests.
close_reason: Fixed by adding range validation (1-10000) for PartNumber via custom deserialization

title: Using i32/i64 where unsigned types are correct
description: ListBucketResult.max_keys (L172), key_count (L174), ListPartsResult.max_parts (L335) use i32 — negative values possible. ObjectInfo.size (L195), PartInfo.size (L356) use i64 — negative sizes possible. Fix: u32 for counts, u64 for sizes.
close_reason: Fixed: changed i32/i64 to u32/u64 for count and size fields respectively

title: to_xml_string pub(crate) hides from cross-crate consumers
description: Both expand_empty_tags and to_xml_string are pub(crate) with #[allow(dead_code)]. Other crates (cirrus-s3, cirrus-router) need to_xml_string for XML responses — they can't call it currently. Blocks ALL Phase 5 handler crates that need XML serialization.
close_reason: Fixed visibility issue: changed to_xml_string and expand_empty_tags from pub(crate) to pub, removed #[allow(dead_code)] attributes. Cross-crate consumers can now access these functions for XML responses.

title: StorageClass as bare String instead of enum
description: ObjectInfo.storage_class and ListPartsResult.storage_class are String (L197, L339). Allows illegal values like 'INVALID_CLASS'. AWS S3 has well-defined set: STANDARD, STANDARD_IA, etc.
close_reason: Fixed by converting storage_class from String to StorageClass enum in types.rs

title: ListBucketResult missing EncodingType field
description: ListBucketResult (L160) missing EncodingType. When request specifies encoding-type=url, field tells clients keys are URL-encoded. Without it, S3 SDKs may misparse keys with special chars.
close_reason: Added EncodingType field to ListBucketResult struct with proper serialization handling. Fix ensures S3 SDKs correctly parse URL-encoded keys when encoding-type=url is specified.

title: DeletedObject and DeleteError missing versioning fields
description: Missing VersionId, DeleteMarker, DeleteMarkerVersionId on DeletedObject (L243) and VersionId on DeleteError (L250). Versioning-aware clients get incomplete responses.
close_reason: Added versioning fields (VersionId, DeleteMarker, DeleteMarkerVersionId) to DeletedObject and VersionId to DeleteError structs with proper serde attributes. Added test cases for both with and without versioning fields. All 39 tests pass.

title: ListPartsResult missing PartNumberMarker
description: ListPartsResult (L323) has NextPartNumberMarker but missing echoed PartNumberMarker. Clients need this for pagination state tracking.
close_reason: Added PartNumberMarker field to ListPartsResult struct and updated tests

title: ObjectInfo missing optional Owner field
description: ObjectInfo (line 186) missing Owner field for ListObjectsV2 when fetch-owner=true. Clients requesting owner info won't receive it.
close_reason: Added owner: Option<Owner> to ObjectInfo struct with skip_serializing_if, + 2 tests verifying with/without Owner XML serialization. cargo build/test/clippy all pass.

title: Missing xmlns namespace on root XML elements
description: Line 115 comment says xmlns 'handled by xml module' but xml.rs is empty stub. All S3 XML responses require `xmlns="http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/"`. Without it, AWS SDKs will reject responses.
close_reason: Implemented xml.rs serialize() with xmlns injection. All 35 tests pass, clippy clean.

title: S3Object content_length can diverge from data.len()
description: Line 89: S3Object stores content_length as independent field from data: Bytes. Nothing prevents mismatch (content_length != data.len()), causing HTTP Content-Length to disagree with body — silent corruption. Fix: remove field, replace with derived method.
close_reason: Removed standalone content_length field, replaced with derived method content_length() returning data.len(). cargo check+test pass, no callers affected.

title: XML comment corruption in expand_empty_tags
description: Line 30: `find('>')` doesn't skip XML comments. A comment like `<!-- <Foo/> -->` would match the `/>` inside, incorrectly expanding it. Needs comment/CDATA/processing-instruction detection.
close_reason: Fixed: expand_empty_tags now detects <!--comments-->, <![CDATA[...]]>, and <?processing-instructions?> before regular tag parsing. 7 regression tests added. All 28 tests pass.

title: UTF-8 corruption in expand_empty_tags
description: Line 53 in types.rs: `bytes[i] as char` corrupts multi-byte UTF-8 sequences (e.g. Unicode S3 keys like '照片/cat.jpg'). The fallthrough casts individual u8 bytes to char, producing garbled chars for non-ASCII text.
close_reason: Refactored expand_empty_tags from byte-index to char-level iteration. Fixed bytes[i] as char corrupting multi-byte UTF-8. 21/21 tests pass (2 new regression tests for Unicode S3 keys). Pushed to feat/cirrus-oo9-types.

title: Cargo.toml: add figment toml feature
close_reason: Added 'toml' feature to figment. Now: features=["env","toml"]. cargo check --workspace passes.

title: Cargo.toml: add quick-xml deserialize feature
close_reason: False positive. quick-xml 0.37 uses a single 'serialize' feature flag that gates BOTH se and de modules. No 'deserialize' feature exists. Existing config is correct.

title: Cargo.toml: add tokio sync and io-util features
close_reason: Added sync and io-util features to tokio deps in Cargo.toml. cargo check --workspace passes.

title: AP-P10: Blocking I/O in Async Handlers
description: DON'T use std::process::Command or other blocking I/O in async handler functions — blocks the async runtime thread. DO: Use pure-Rust crypto crates (md-5, sha2) for MD5/SHA256 computations. All cryptographic operations are CPU-bound but small enough to run on async threads without blocking.

title: AP-P9: Per-Request State in Static Variables
description: DON'T use static AtomicU64 for request IDs or other per-request state — not thread-safe for unique IDs and prevents multiple instances. DO: Use uuid::Uuid::new_v4() for each request. Generate once in dispatch, thread through handlers. UUID v4 is stateless and doesn't need atomics.

title: AP-P8: Self-Closing XML Elements
description: DON'T let quick-xml emit <Element/> for empty string values — AWS S3 uses explicit <Element></Element>. DO: For hand-built responses always write both open+close tags. For quick-xml serde, test with empty strings, use custom serializers if needed to force explicit tags.

title: AP-P7: EntityTooLarge Returning HTTP 413
description: DON'T return bare HTTP 413 from tower_http::limit::RequestBodyLimitLayer — AWS S3 returns EntityTooLarge as HTTP 400 with XML body. DO: Intercept the 413 in middleware, convert to AwsError::EntityTooLarge which returns HTTP 400 with proper AWS XML error body.

title: AP-P6: Bytes::clone() Memory Accounting for CopyObject
description: WATCH OUT: Bytes::clone() is O(1) Arc refcount bump, NOT a memory duplicate. DON'T increment total_bytes on CopyObject — the underlying memory is shared with the source object. Only increment total_bytes on PutObject, UploadPart, and CompleteMultipartUpload.

title: AP-P5: Skipping URL Decoding on Path Segments
description: DON'T use raw path segments without decoding — 'hello%20world' stays encoded, breaking Unicode keys and URL-encoded characters. DO: Always URL-decode path segments via urlencoding::decode(). This is a known bug pattern that breaks keys with spaces, Unicode, and special chars.

title: AP-P4: New Tokio Runtime Per Benchmark
description: DON'T create a new tokio::runtime::Runtime per benchmark function — adds runtime creation overhead to measurements and wastes memory. DO: Use a single tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread() shared runtime across all benchmarks via b.to_async(&rt).

title: AP-P3: RwLock Delete Guard for DeleteBucket
description: DON'T use RwLock<bool> delete_guard in Bucket — creates ABBA deadlock between delete_guard and DashMap shard locks. DO: Use DashMap's atomic remove() to isolate the bucket, then check objects.is_empty() && multipart_uploads.is_empty(). Re-insert bucket on BucketNotEmpty failure. Single lock ordering: parent DashMap lock first, then bucket internals.

title: AP-P2: Deriving Clone on DashMap Structs
description: DON'T #[derive(Clone)] on structs containing DashMap — DashMap doesn't impl Clone, compile error. DO: Wrap DashMap in Arc, implement Clone manually using Arc::clone() for both fields. DefaultStorage uses Arc<DashMap<String, Bucket>> and Arc<AtomicU64>.

title: AP-P1: Global/Static Service Registry
description: DON'T use static SERVICE_REGISTRY: Lazy<ServiceRegistry> — prevents multiple server instances in tests. DO: Pass via axum's State extractor with Arc<ServiceRegistry> and .with_state(). This ensures testability and no global mutable state.

title: Phase 7: Binary Entry Point (cirrus-server)
description: Build cirrus-server binary: CLI arg parsing with clap (--port, --bind, --log-level), config loading with Figment (env CIRRUS_* > CLI overrides), JSON tracing setup with tracing-subscriber, server assembly (DefaultStorage -> S3Service -> ServiceRegistry -> build_router), tokio TCP bind on config addr, axum::serve with graceful shutdown (SIGTERM + Ctrl+C). NO global state. multi_thread tokio runtime. CIRRUS_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID as informational config.

title: Phase 6: Error Handling & Response Standardization
description: Implement s3_error_to_aws_error() mapping function covering all 14 S3Error->AwsError conversions with correct HTTP status (§6.2). Generate request_id (UUID v4) once per request in S3Service::handle(), thread through all handlers. Verify 501 NotImplemented returns valid AWS XML for non-S3 services. Validate all specific error XML schemas (§6.3): InvalidArgument with ArgumentName/ArgumentValue, BucketNotEmpty with BucketName, InvalidPart with UploadId/PartNumber/ETag. Ensure x-amz-request-id and x-amz-id-2 on EVERY response. Verify edge cases: empty Delete body returns 200 with empty DeleteResult, empty CreateBucketConfiguration body is valid.

title: Phase 5e: Multipart Upload Operations
description: Implement: handle_create_multipart_upload() - Generate uploadId via base64url(sha256(bucket:key:timestamp:random)), return InitiateMultipartUploadResult XML; handle_upload_part() - validate partNumber 1-10000, compute ETag, return 200 with ETag header; handle_complete_multipart_upload() - parse CompleteMultipartUpload XML, validate parts+etags, compute composite ETag (md5 of concatenated data + '-N'), return result; handle_abort_multipart_upload() - return 204; handle_list_parts() - paginate with part-number-marker, return ListPartsResult XML with Initiator/Owner/Part entries. Plus multipart.rs UploadId generation helper.

title: Phase 5d: Batch & Copy Operations (handlers)
description: Implement handler functions: handle_delete_objects() - parse Delete XML, extract Object/Key elements, sequentially delete per key, respect Quiet flag, return DeleteResult XML; handle_copy_object() - extract x-amz-copy-source (URL-decode AFTER dispatch), parse src bucket/key, handle x-amz-metadata-directive (COPY/REPLACE), return CopyObjectResult XML with ETag+LastModified. Non-transactional batch delete per spec.

title: Phase 5c: Object Operations (handlers)
description: Implement handler functions: handle_put_object() - extract Content-Type, Content-Length, x-amz-meta-* headers, compute MD5 ETag, return 200 with ETag header; handle_get_object() - return 200 with object bytes and headers (Content-Type, Content-Length, ETag, Last-Modified, Accept-Ranges); handle_head_object() - return headers only, empty body; handle_delete_object() - return 204 (idempotent, 404 only if bucket missing).

title: Phase 5b: Bucket Operations (handlers)
description: Implement handler functions: handle_list_buckets() - ListAllMyBucketsResult XML, Owner 000000000000/webfile; handle_create_bucket() - validate name 3-63 chars, parse optional CreateBucketConfiguration, return 200 with Location header; handle_delete_bucket() - return 204 No Content; handle_get_bucket_location() - return LocationConstraint with us-east-1.

title: Phase 5a: S3 Dispatch & Routing (service.rs)
description: Implement S3Service<S>::handle() for AwsService trait. Inside handle(): resolve_address to get bucket+key, match on (method, bucket, key, query) to dispatch to 16 operations. Rules from §4.1: GET /, PUT/{bucket}, DELETE/{bucket}, GET?list-type=2, PUT with x-amz-copy-source (check BEFORE URL decode), PUT without copy-source, GET/{key}, HEAD/{key}, DELETE/{key}, POST?delete, POST?uploads, PUT?partNumber+uploadId, POST?uploadId, DELETE?uploadId, GET?uploadId, GET?location. Default -> MethodNotAllowed.

title: Phase 4c: Middleware Layer & Router Assembly
description: Build complete axum Router with: tower::ServiceBuilder middleware composition, RequestBodyLimitLayer with custom 400 EntityTooLarge conversion (anti-pattern P7 fix), IncompleteBody detection middleware (compares bytes read vs Content-Length), graceful fallback handler via ServiceRegistry, and with_state(Arc<ServiceRegistry>). No global/static state (anti-pattern P1 fix).

title: Phase 4b: S3 Address Resolution & SigV4 Extraction
description: Implement extract_service() from SigV4 Authorization header, X-Amz-Target, or ?Service= query param (default 's3'). Implement resolve_address() for path-style and virtual-hosted S3 addressing with URL decoding via urlencoding crate. CRITICAL: URL-decode path segments to handle %20, %2F, etc. (anti-pattern P5 fix).

title: Phase 4a: ServiceRegistry, AwsService Trait & Fallback
description: Define ServiceRegistry (HashMap<String, Box<dyn AwsService>>) with register() method. Define #[async_trait] AwsService trait with handle(). Implement fallback_handler that extracts service from request, looks up in registry, delegates or returns AwsError::NotImplemented (501) with valid AWS XML.
close_reason: Phase 4a complete: AwsService trait, ServiceRegistry (DashMap-backed), and fallback_handler (axum) implemented in cirrus-router. Routes unmatched requests to registered services, returns 501 NotImplemented XML for unknown services. 11 tests passing, workspace compiles clean.

title: Phase 3: Storage Data Structures, Trait & DefaultStorage
description: Define Bucket, S3Object, MultipartUpload, response types (ObjectList, GetObjectResult, PartsList, etc.), S3Error enum, Storage trait with 16 methods, DefaultStorage with Arc<DashMap>+Arc<AtomicU64>, manual Clone impl, S3Service generic wrapper, and all trait method implementations including: create/delete/list buckets, put/get/head/delete/copy objects, create/upload/complete/abort multipart uploads, list parts, list objects v2 with pagination, get bucket location. Memory management with CIRRUS_MAX constants.
notes: PR #5 review findings: cirrus-s3t (P1 bug) RefMut hold in complete_multipart_upload; cirrus-5os (P1 bug) delete_bucket re-insertion race; cirrus-x6r (P2 bug) list_objects_v2 panic risk; cirrus-1fz (P3 task) style nits; cirrus-6sn (P3 task) test naming mismatch
close_reason: Phase 3 storage implementation merged to main via PR #5. 1634 lines in storage.rs with Storage trait (16 methods), DefaultStorage, 32 tests. All 5 review findings resolved. Closes AP bugs cirrus-wvw, cirrus-41k, cirrus-n2i.

title: Phase 2c: XML Utilities & Helpers (xml.rs)
description: Implement xml_escape() (5 XML entities), format_timestamp() (ISO 8601 with ms), format_http_date() (IMF-fixdate RFC 7231), format_etag() (quoted hex MD5). Include consistency_check() tests for escape round-trips, date format patterns, and ETag format validation.
close_reason: Implemented XML utilities: xml_escape, format_timestamp, format_http_date, format_etag, and serialize functions with comprehensive tests

title: Phase 2b: AwsError Enum & XML Serialization (error.rs)
description: Define AwsError enum with all variants (NotImplemented, NoSuchBucket, NoSuchKey, NoSuchUpload, etc.). Implement to_xml(), status_code() mapping per §6.2, error_code() strings, IntoResponse for axum. Include request_id/host_id fields with builder/setter pattern. Hand-build small error responses with format!()+xml_escape() for performance.
notes: Feature branch squash merged into main. Implementation complete: AwsError enum with XML serialization, builder pattern, and all required methods implemented. Fixed critical bug: missing XML namespace in Error responses.
close_reason: Implementation complete: AwsError enum with XML serialization, builder pattern, and all required methods implemented

title: Phase 2a: Shared Types & XML Schemas (types.rs)
description: Define Owner, S3Object, and all 10 XML response structs (ListAllMyBucketsResult, BucketInfo, CreateBucketOutput, ListBucketResult, Delete+DeleteResult, CopyObjectResult, InitiateMultipartUploadResult, CompleteMultipartUpload+Result, ListPartsResult, LocationConstraint). All with quick-xml/serde Serialize derive. Handle empty elements as '<E></E>' not '<E/>'.
close_reason: Implement types.rs with Owner, S3Object, 11 XML struct families, 19 tests. Build/test/clippy all pass. Key finding: quick-xml 0.37 serializes empty strings as self-closing <Tag/> — fixed via expand_empty_tags() post-processor.

title: Phase 1: Project Scaffold & Crate Structure
description: Create Cargo workspace with all 4 crates (cirrus-protocol, cirrus-s3, cirrus-router, cirrus-server), dependency declarations via workspace inheritance, .gitignore, rust-toolchain.toml, .editorconfig, and stub lib.rs/main.rs files. Verify: cargo build --workspace compiles with empty stubs and cargo clippy passes.
close_reason: Phase 1 scaffold complete: workspace, 4 crates, deps via workspace inheritance, rust-toolchain, .gitignore, .editorconfig. All builds/passes/lint verified.

title: AP-P13: register() silently overwrites existing services
notes: DON'T silently overwrite registered services via DashMap::insert() — misconfiguration or duplicate init swaps working service with no warning. DO: Return Result from register(), or at least tracing::warn! on overwrite. Fix location: crates/cirrus-router/src/service.rs L87-89. PR#6 review finding (bug-hunter).
close_reason: Warn on overwrite: register() checks DashMap insert return value, emits tracing::warn! if overwriting existing service

title: AP-P12: Error response leaks service registration state — enables enumeration
notes: DON'T echo user-controlled service_name in host_id error field — enables service enumeration via probing. DO: Replace with generic 'Service unavailable' message. Fix location: crates/cirrus-router/src/service.rs L163. Note: XML injection mitigated by escape_xml() in cirrus-protocol/src/error.rs but info leakage remains. PR#6 review finding (security-auditor).
close_reason: Replaced  echoing service_name with generic . Added  for server-side visibility. Removed info leakage vector for service enumeration.

title: AP-P14: aws_error_response uses .expect() — panic surface in library code
notes: DON'T use .expect() in library code paths — panics if Response::builder fails (e.g., invalid header value). DO: Replace with unwrap_or_else that returns a fallback 500 response with logging. Fix location: crates/cirrus-router/src/service.rs L181. PR#6 review finding (security-auditor + code-quality-reviewer).

title: list_objects_v2 potential panic on concurrent key mutation
close_reason: Fixed: replaced indexing slice &key[prefix.len()..] with key.get(prefix.len()..).unwrap_or('') to eliminate panic risk. Defensive fix — keys are eagerly collected with starts_with filter so no active panic path exists, but .get() removes refactoring risk. cargo check/clippy/test all pass.

title: Fix inject_xmlns function to prevent duplicate xmlns attributes
description: Fix the inject_xmlns function in crates/cirrus-protocol/src/xml.rs to properly handle cases where the root element already has an xmlns attribute, preventing duplicate xmlns attributes which create invalid XML. The function should ensure exactly one xmlns attribute with the correct S3 namespace value on the root element. Update the test case test_inject_xmlns_does_not_duplicate() to verify correct behavior.

title: Fix PartNumber range validation (1-10000) in cirrus-protocol
description: Part.part_number in crates/cirrus-protocol/src/types.rs line 498 allows values outside 1-10000 range. S3 spec requires part numbers 1-10000. Type currently permits construction of invalid requests. Add validation to ensure part_number is in range 1-10000 inclusive.
close_reason: Implemented PartNumber range validation (1-10000) using custom deserialization. Added S3Error type and comprehensive unit tests.

title: Phase 9b: CI/CD Pipeline (GitHub Actions)
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml with 3 jobs: test (cargo test, clippy -D warnings, fmt --check), integration (needs: test, install awscli+boto3 via pip, cargo build, CIRRUS_INTEGRATION_TESTS=1 cargo test --test integration), build (if tag push, needs: test, cargo build --release, docker build, push to GHCR). Use actions-rust-lang/setup-rust-toolchain@v1, actions/checkout@v4.

title: Phase 9a: Docker Build
description: Create multi-stage Dockerfile: build stage with rust:1.85-alpine + musl-dev, target x86_64-unknown-linux-musl release build; runtime stage with gcr.io/distroless/static-debian12 minimal image. Support ARG RUST_TARGET for multi-platform. Include --mount=type=cache for cargo registry. Create .dockerignore excluding target/, .git/, benches/, tests/, *.md. Verify binary <25 MB, image <50 MB, startup <20 ms in container, idle RSS <8 MiB.

title: Phase 8d: Benchmarks (criterion)
description: Create benches/s3_benchmark.rs using criterion: bench_put_object_1mb (pre-create bucket, put 1 MB object), bench_get_object_1mb (pre-populate 1 MB object, get it), bench_list_objects_100 (pre-populate 100 objects, list them). Use single tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread() shared across benchmarks (anti-pattern P4 fix). Use black_box() to prevent compiler optimization. Log results for regression tracking.

title: Phase 8c: Property-Based Tests (proptest)
description: Write proptest-based tests: generate random Unicode strings, encode as URL-path segments, resolve via resolve_address, verify decoded value matches original; generate random keys, put and get, verify keys stored literally (S3 treats keys as opaque strings); generate random metadata key/value pairs with embedded special chars, put and retrieve, verify metadata round-trips with \r\n stripped per spec. Run 10,000+ test cases for key encoding.

title: Phase 8b: Integration Test Harness & Tests
description: Build with_server() harness that spawns real server on random port. Write integration tests: create bucket -> list buckets, put object -> get object (bytes round-trip), put object -> head object (ETag match), delete object -> get object 404, batch delete objects, multipart (create->upload 3 parts->complete->get, create->upload 1->abort), list objects with prefix/delimiter/max-keys, copy object, non-existent bucket/key 404 XML, missing list-type 400 InvalidArgument XML, EntityTooLarge 400 XML, non-S3 service 501 XML.

title: Phase 8a: MockStorage & Unit Tests
description: Implement MockStorage under #[cfg(test)] in storage.rs using Arc<Mutex<HashMap>>. Write storage unit tests: bucket lifecycle (create/list/delete), object CRUD (put/get/head/delete), concurrent ops, multipart lifecycle (create->upload 3 parts->complete->get, create->upload 1->abort->empty), ListObjectsV2 pagination (100 keys, max-keys=10), prefix+delimiter, CopyObject same/cross-bucket, CopyObject REPLACE metadata, memory management (EntityTooLarge), DeleteBucket with/without objects, bucket name validation. Write handler unit tests with MockStorage verifying HTTP status codes, XML response bodies, and all response headers.

title: test_complete_multipart_upload_wrong_order tests duplicates not wrong order
description:
test_complete_multipart_upload_wrong_order (L1339-1376) sends [Part{1, etag1}, Part{1, etag1}] — duplicate part numbers — which triggers InvalidPartOrder because 1 <= 1. But the test name says "wrong order."

A true wrong-order test would be [Part{2, etag2}, Part{1, etag1}].

**Fix options:**
1. Rename test to test_complete_multipart_upload_duplicate_parts
2. Add a separate test for actual wrong order: [Part{2}, Part{1}]
3. Both

**Location:** crates/cirrus-s3/src/storage.rs L1339-1376

Discovered during PR #5 review. Depends on cirrus-qhv.
close_reason: Renamed test_complete_multipart_upload_wrong_order to test_complete_multipart_upload_duplicate_parts (line 1353). Added new test_complete_multipart_upload_wrong_order that tests actual reversed order [Part{2}, Part{1}]. 32/32 tests pass. All PR #5 review findings now fixed.

title: storage.rs: redundant drop(upload) and indentation nit
description:
Two minor style nits in storage.rs:

1. **L788: redundant drop(upload)** — The RefMut is already dropped at end of scope. The explicit drop() is correct but the comment implies it is needed for correctness when it is not. Keep for readability or remove to reduce noise.

2. **L867: inconsistent indentation** — The .map() call in list_parts has extra indentation (16 spaces instead of 12):
```rust
        let part_infos: Vec<PartInfo> = all_matching[..take]
            .iter()
                .map(|(pn, p)| PartInfo {  // extra indent
```

Discovered during PR #5 review. Depends on cirrus-qhv.
close_reason: Both style nits fixed: removed redundant drop(upload) in complete_multipart_upload (block scope handles drop), fixed indentation in list_parts .map() call. Commit 4f1349e on feat/cirrus-qhv-storage. Verified: cargo check/clippy/test all pass (31/31).

title: Phase 10: Documentation & Polish
description: Create README.md with Installation, Quick Start (cargo install, cirrus-server, aws s3api examples), Supported Operations table (16 operations from §4.1), Configuration env vars table (§8.1). Create CHANGELOG.md in Keep a Changelog format with v0.1.0 entry. Verify Definition of Done checklist (§16): all 16 ops tested >85% coverage, integration tests pass, musl binary <25 MB, Docker <50 MB, startup <50ms/<20ms, idle RSS <8 MiB, CI passing, no unwrap()/expect() in production code, no todo!()/unimplemented!(), no global mutable state.
